paper.title: Spatio-Temporal Token Pruning for Efficient High-Resolution GUI Agents
paper.abstract: Pure-vision GUI agents provide universal interaction capabilities but suffer from severe efficiency bottlenecks due to the massive spatiotemporal redundancy inherent in high-resolution screenshots and historical trajectories. We identify two critical misalignments in existing compression paradigms: the temporal mismatch, where uniform history encoding diverges from the agent's "fading memory" attention pattern, and the spatial topology conflict, where unstructured pruning compromises the grid in

memory_insight.peak_dram_usage: 3.4x reduction in FLOPs and 3.3x speedup in vision encoding latency
memory_insight.compression_quantization_methods: Temporal-Adaptive Resolution (TAR) and Stratified Structure-aware Pruning (SSP)

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing Temporal-Adaptive Resolution (TAR) and Stratified Structure-aware Pruning (SSP) can significantly reduce memory usage and improve performance in high-resolution GUI navigation models.
technical_details: Grid integrity required for precise coordinate grounding
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices with 8-16GB RAM, as it achieves a 3.4x reduction in FLOPs and a 3.3x speedup in vision encoding latency.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
